surface-zebra.note: Subtle alternating-row tint for the holdings + watchlist register rows (~1.5% delta from surface in light, ~3.5% in dark). Dark mode pair: oklch(21.5% 0.008 60) — bumped 2026-05-14 from 20.5% so the alternation reads in dark too. Added 2026-05-13 with the register-row primitive.

display.purpose: Hero USD total only. One per page.

headline.purpose: Section anchors when prose introduces a block.

title.purpose: Card headings, ticker names, drill-in subheaders.

body.purpose: Default reading text. Insight prose, anomaly translations, position notes.

label.purpose: Recurring section caption. The single most-used type role.

numeric.purpose: Tabular figures via .tabular class on every numeric cell.

mono.purpose: Inline <code> only. Numbers do not switch to mono.

motion.name: transition-colors
motion.purpose: The only transition in the system. Applied to row hover, nav tab hover, theme toggle hover, sortable header hover, textarea focus.

motion.name: reduced-motion
motion.value: all transitions and animations zeroed via prefers-reduced-motion: reduce

components.name: Section caption (label cap)
components.description: The recurring uppercase caption that opens every block. The single most-used type role in the system; recurrence is the affordance.
components.css: .ds-label-cap { font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(45% 0.008 60); margin-bottom: 0.75rem; }

components.name: Sortable table header
components.description: The header cell trigger inside holdings + watchlist tables. Three-click cycle: desc, asc, cleared. Persists in localStorage.
components.css: .ds-sortable-header { font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.04em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(58% 0.007 65); background: none; border: 0; padding: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: color 200ms; } .ds-sortable-header:hover { color: oklch(20% 0.008 60); } .ds-sortable-header-active { color: oklch(20% 0.008 60); } .ds-sortable-indicator { margin-left: 0.25rem; color: oklch(45% 0.008 60); }

components.name: Register row (holdings table)
components.description: Holdings table row as a register row primitive (introduced 2026-05-13). Dedicated leftmost glyph margin column (w-6, always present even when empty) stacks the earnings ⊙ and ex-dividend ƒ marks. Body rows: border-b border-rule, py-3 (~25% tighter than the prior py-4). Even rows tint to surface-zebra (~1.5% delta from surface) via the `even:` Tailwind variant; hover and expanded override the zebra base. Numeric columns use font-medium + tabular; secondary captions inside the same cell drop to font-normal + text-whisper so the primary figure carries the row.
components.html:
ƒ
MU
Micron Technology · NASDAQ · USD
↑ +5.2%
INTC
Intel Corp · NASDAQ · USD
↓ −0.9%
⊙
ANET
Arista Networks · NYSE · USD
↑ +0.4%
components.css: .ds-register { width: 100%; border-collapse: collapse; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-register-row { border-bottom: 1px solid oklch(86% 0.006 70); } .ds-register-row-zebra { background: oklch(94.5% 0.006 75); } .ds-register-glyph { width: 1.5rem; padding: 0.75rem 0.5rem 0.75rem 0.25rem; text-align: center; color: oklch(45% 0.008 60); vertical-align: top; } .ds-glyph-exdiv { font-family: Georgia, serif; font-style: italic; font-size: 0.875rem; } .ds-glyph-earnings { font-size: 0.875rem; } .ds-register-name { padding: 0.75rem 1rem 0.75rem 0; vertical-align: top; } .ds-register-ticker { font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 500; color: oklch(20% 0.008 60); line-height: 1.2; } .ds-register-name-meta { font-size: 0.75rem; color: oklch(45% 0.008 60); margin-top: 0.125rem; } .ds-register-figure { padding: 0.75rem 1rem; text-align: right; font-feature-settings: 'tnum' 1; font-variant-numeric: tabular-nums; font-weight: 500; vertical-align: top; } .ds-register-gain { color: oklch(48% 0.10 145); } .ds-register-loss { color: oklch(48% 0.13 25); }

components.name: Drill-in container
components.description: Closest thing to a card in the system. Full-bleed inside the table row that expanded it. Two-column grid with chart left, insight + notes + anomalies right.
components.html:
Last 90 days
What this means
Plain-English insight goes here, two short sentences, no jargon.
components.css: .ds-drill-in { background: oklch(94% 0.008 75); border-top: 1px solid oklch(86% 0.006 70); padding: 1.5rem; } .ds-drill-in-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr; gap: 2rem; } @media (min-width: 1024px) { .ds-drill-in-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr 360px; } } .ds-label-cap { font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(45% 0.008 60); margin-bottom: 0.5rem; }

components.name: Notes textarea
components.description: The only input in the system. Debounced auto-save (800ms idle). Empty placeholder is italic whisper text. Focus shifts the border to the muted rust accent.
components.html:
Notes
Last saved · 30s ago
components.css: .ds-label-cap { font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.75rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.06em; text-transform: uppercase; color: oklch(45% 0.008 60); margin-bottom: 0.75rem; } .ds-textarea-notes { width: 100%; background: oklch(98% 0.004 75); border: 1px solid oklch(86% 0.006 70); border-radius: 2px; padding: 0.5rem 0.75rem; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.875rem; line-height: 1.55; color: oklch(20% 0.008 60); resize: vertical; transition: border-color 200ms; } .ds-textarea-notes::placeholder { color: oklch(58% 0.007 65); font-style: italic; } .ds-textarea-notes:focus { outline: none; border-color: oklch(55% 0.12 28); } .ds-textarea-status { font-size: 0.75rem; color: oklch(58% 0.007 65); margin-top: 0.375rem; height: 1rem; }

components.name: Navigation tab strip
components.description: Single header row across all routes. Lowercase tabs with a pb-1 border-b underline; active tab inks both the text and the underline.
components.html:
quiet ledger
homeportfoliowatchlist
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: baseline; justify-content: space-between; margin-bottom: 3rem; font-family: 'IBM Plex Sans', system-ui, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-group { display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 2rem; } .ds-nav-masthead { font-size: 0.875rem; font-weight: 500; letter-spacing: 0.025em; color: oklch(45% 0.008 60); } .ds-nav-tabs { display: flex; align-items: baseline; gap: 1.25rem; font-size: 0.75rem; } .ds-nav-tab { padding-bottom: 0.25rem; border-bottom: 1px solid transparent; color: oklch(45% 0.008 60); text-decoration: none; transition: color 200ms; } .ds-nav-tab:hover { color: oklch(20% 0.008 60); } .ds-nav-tab-active { color: oklch(20% 0.008 60); border-bottom-color: oklch(20% 0.008 60); } .ds-theme-toggle { font-size: 0.75rem; text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.05em; color: oklch(45% 0.008 60); background: none; border: 0; cursor: pointer; transition: color 200ms; } .ds-theme-toggle:hover { color: oklch(20% 0.008 60); }

components.name: Sparkline (30-day)
components.description: Hand-rolled SVG path, 96x28 viewBox. Stroke color is the gain, loss, or quiet token depending on net direction over the window. SSR-renderable.
components.html: <svg class="ds-sparkline" width="96" height="28" viewBox="0 0 96 28" role="img" aria-label="30-day price trend, gain"><path d="M2,22 L5,20 L8,18 L11,19 L14,17 L17,15 L20,16 L23,14 L26,13 L29,15 L32,12 L35,11 L38,12 L41,10 L44,9 L47,11 L50,8 L53,7 L56,9 L59,6 L62,5 L65,7 L68,4 L71,6 L74,3 L77,5 L80,2 L83,4 L86,6 L89,3 L92,5 L94,4" fill="none" stroke="oklch(48% 0.10 145)" stroke-width="1.25" stroke-linecap="round" stroke-linejoin="round" /></svg>

components.name: Calendar mark
components.description: Inline 12px outlined-stroke calendar glyph next to a ticker name when earnings is within 14 days. currentColor follows theme; title attribute carries the date.
components.css: .ds-calendar-mark { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; color: oklch(45% 0.008 60); cursor: help; }

narrative.overview:
The dashboard is a paper-and-ink ledger for a long-horizon investor, a place for sitting with positions, not reacting to them. Warm graphite type rests on cream in light mode; in dark mode the same family inverts to paper-cream ink on a warm-graphite surface. The accent is rare on purpose. Numbers are reported with the same typographic gravity at 0.2% as at 5%; the data does the talking, not the chrome. Mercury for restraint, Linear for craft, Notion for the canvas-as-page feel.

Explicitly not a Bloomberg full-clone, not Robinhood, not the generic LLM SaaS dashboard. The reading room rejects all three by construction.

Three thin server-component routes (/, /portfolio, /watchlist) share a single max-w-6xl column, a NavBar tab strip in pb-1 border-b underline form, and a theme toggle that cycles system → light → dark → system. Tokens are CSS custom properties in web/src/app/globals.css; light is default, dark inverts under the .dark class. Both modes are first-class.
keyCharacteristics: Paper-cream surface, warm-graphite ink, one rare rust accent.
One humanist sans family (IBM Plex Sans + Plex Mono), tabular figures opt-in on numeric cells.
Flat by default; depth from spacing, hairline borders, and tonal contrast within the warm-neutral family.
Two densities (glance, study) sharing one visual vocabulary; drill-in not switch-of-surface is how density scales.
Color never carries meaning alone; gain/loss always paired with arrow + sign.
Charts that ship in SSR HTML are hand-rolled SVG; Recharts only inside lazy-mounted drill-ins.
Restrained motion: state changes only; prefers-reduced-motion: reduce zeroes all transitions and animations globally.

rules.name: The OKLCH Doctrine
rules.body: Every color in the system is OKLCH, even where it triggers a Stitch linter warning on the frontmatter. The surface family lives in a narrow chroma band (0.005-0.013) so neutrals visibly belong to the warm cream rather than drift into cool gray.

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: The accent is used on ≤10% of any screen. Its rarity is the message. Two accents on one view is one accent too many.

rules.name: The No-Green-On-Red Rule
rules.body: Gain and loss are never communicated by color alone. Every up/down value carries an arrow (↑/↓), explicit sign (+/−), or positional cue. Color is reinforcement, never the signal.

rules.name: The Tinted-Neutral Rule
rules.body: Pure black and pure white are forbidden. Every neutral is tinted toward the warm surface family with chroma 0.005-0.01.

rules.name: The Dark-Mode Parity Rule
rules.body: Dark is not a reskin and not an afterthought. Every token has a paired dark value, every component reads on both surfaces, and the theme toggle cycles system → light → dark → system.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: Every glyph in the interface comes from IBM Plex Sans (or Plex Mono for inline code) at varying weights.

rules.name: The Tabular-Numbers Rule
rules.body: All numeric data uses tabular figures. Body text keeps tabular off so prose reads naturally; the .tabular class is opt-in on every numeric cell, header, and data caption.

rules.name: The Same-Gravity Rule
rules.body: A 0.2% move and a 5% move share the same typographic treatment. Magnitude is read from the digits, not from size.

rules.name: The Lowercase-Chrome Rule
rules.body: Navigation tabs and chrome labels render lowercase. The label cap above each block is the only uppercase element in the system.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces have no shadow at rest. If a hover state ever introduces shadow, it is hairline and reserved for genuinely interactive elements.

rules.name: The No-Floating-Cards Rule
rules.body: Cards are not the default container. Most things are sections divided by spacing and a hairline rule. The drill-in is the closest thing to a card and is full-bleed within the row it expands from.

rules.name: The Color-Only Transitions Rule
rules.body: Transitions animate color and opacity. Never transform, never layout properties. Numbers do not animate in or out; they update.

rules.name: The Hand-Rolled-SVG Rule
rules.body: Charts that ship in SSR HTML are hand-rolled SVG (sparkline, donut, concentration stack, currency stack, benchmark line). Recharts is reserved for lazy-mounted drill-in surfaces.

rules.name: The Same-Caption Rule
rules.body: Every block opens with the text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.06em] text-quiet caption. Recurrence is the affordance.

rules.name: The Drill-In-Not-Modal Rule
rules.body: A row's depth is reached by expanding inline, not by opening a modal or navigating to a sub-page.

dos: Do keep the accent under 10% of any screen. Its rarity is the point.
Do pair every gain/loss color with an arrow and explicit sign. Color is reinforcement, never the signal.
Do add the .tabular class to every numeric cell, header, and caption. Body text stays non-tabular.
Do tint every neutral toward the warm surface family. Chroma 0.005-0.01. No #000. No #fff.
Do treat a 5% move and a 0.2% move with the same typographic gravity.
Do scale glance-density to study-density by drilling in, not by switching to a different layout.
Do keep prose at 65-75ch line length where prose runs long.
Do reach for full borders or background tint when separation is needed.
Do ship hand-rolled SVG for any chart that lives in SSR HTML; reserve Recharts for lazy-mounted drill-ins.
Do open every block with the text-xs uppercase tracking-[0.06em] text-quiet caption. The recurrence is the affordance.
Do keep advisor copy plain-English: never buy, sell, hold, trim, add, target, forecast, predict, recommend, you should, rally, surge, soar, crash.
donts: Don't become a Bloomberg / MarketWatch full-clone. Twenty widgets, blinking tickers, every metric possible.
Don't use the crypto neon-on-black hype look. Hot pink / cyan on near-black, glow effects.
Don't become Robinhood. No gotcha-green, no candy gradients, no confetti, no big-number-flex hero.
Don't ship the generic LLM-default SaaS dashboard. No Inter, no slate-blue, no cards-nested-in-cards.
Don't use border-left greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards or alerts.
Don't apply background-clip: text with a gradient. Use a single solid color, with emphasis through weight.
Don't apply backdrop-filter: blur decoratively. No glassmorphism unless purposeful and rare.
Don't build a hero-metric template (huge number, small label, supporting stats, gradient accent).
Don't repeat identical card grids. Same-sized cards with icon + heading + text endlessly is a layout failure.
Don't reach for a modal as the first thought. Exhaust inline and progressive (drill-in) alternatives first.
Don't animate prices in or out. Numbers updating is the data doing its job.
Don't animate transform or layout. Color and opacity only, ≤200ms.
Don't use bounce or elastic easing. Ease out with exponential curves.
Don't use em dashes in copy. Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or parentheses.
Don't introduce a second sans family. One Plex Sans for everything; Plex Mono only for inline code.
